key_insight: A lone-pair-rich hybrid perovskite, GuaPbI3, creates a chemically soft lattice that strongly suppresses phonon transport and yields ultralow thermal conductivity.
engineering_relevance: This points to a materials-design route for thermoelectric coolers and thermal insulators that targets lattice softness rather than relying only on nanostructuring.
method: Physics-guided symbolic regression to identify lone-pair-dominated chemistries
DFT plus transport and impedance analysis to link structure, phonons, and charge transport
evidence_signal: The abstract reports room-temperature thermal conductivity of 0.088 W m^-1 K^-1, with bulk-dominated, bias-dependent electrical conduction confirmed by impedance spectroscopy.
watch_item: The very low thermal conductivity does not guarantee practical device performance, since carrier transport appears sensitive to chemical potential and may be morphology- or bias-dependent.
paper_title: Lone-Pair-Induced Lattice Softness Enables Ultralow Thermal Conductivity in Hybrid Organic-Inorganic Perovskite GuaPbI$_3$
